title: M+M Explore Travel Bundle
description: Schema for the travel.json consumed by the M+M Explore PWA. One bundle describes exactly one trip.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema so IDEs can validate / autocomplete.

schema_version.description: Bundle schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

chargers.description: Reusable EV charger entities. Stops can reference these via Stop.charger_id to avoid duplicating coords/operator/kw across multiple drives.

weather.description: Daily weather forecast per stay, written by the weather GitHub Action. Keys are stay ids (matching stays[].id). PWA renders weather for a given Day by finding all stays whose [check_in, check_out] range covers the date.

Theme.description: CSS custom property overrides. All optional; app falls back to defaults.

primary.description: Dominant brand color (active tabs, primary buttons).

title.description: Short label shown in day picker / app.

from.description: Display name of origin (e.g. 'Tropea'). Prefer also setting from_place_id when the origin is a known Place.

to.description: Display name of destination. Prefer also setting to_place_id when the destination is a known Place.

from_place_id.description: Optional explicit reference to a Place by id. Authoritative when present; avoids fragile name matching for route rendering.

to_place_id.description: Optional explicit reference to a Place by id. For TBD destinations (e.g. a not-yet-decided candidate location), leave unset and use status='candidate' on the concrete per-candidate drive entries instead.

status.description: Required. 'confirmed' for the active plan. 'candidate' marks an unconfirmed drive (renders dashed). 'completed' marks a drive that has already happened (renders muted). 'cancelled' marks a planned drive that won't happen (hidden by default).

duration_min.description: Total duration including stops, in minutes.

maps_url.description: Fallback navigation URL if coords unavailable.

coords.description: Destination coordinates for dynamic maps URL generation.

note.description: Short operator note shown on the drive card.

Stop.description: A waypoint or alternative on a Drive. Must anchor via at least one of: charger_id (Charger reference), place_id (Place reference), or inline name+coords.

type.description: Defaults to 'charge' if omitted. EV fields (kw, soc_*) are only meaningful when type is 'charge'.

role.description: Defaults to 'stop' if omitted. 'stop' entries are confirmed waypoints — the route polyline passes through them in array order. 'alternative' entries are optional Plan-B options near the route — they appear as muted pins on the map but do NOT affect the polyline.

place_id.description: Optional reference to places[].id. When set, Place data (coords, POIs, notes) is the source of truth and Stop fields like coords/note act as overrides only. Use for Sight/Stay-anchored stops; for chargers use charger_id instead.

charger_id.description: Optional reference to chargers[].id. When set, Charger data (coords, operator, kw, stalls) is the source of truth and Stop fields act as overrides only. Mutually disjoint from place_id (a Stop is either a Place- or Charger-anchor, not both).

kw.description: Charger peak power in kW. Only meaningful for type 'charge'.

place_id.description: References places[].id — used for Erleben mode grouping.

check_in.description: Date-only acceptable when check-in time is not yet known (direct bookings). DateTime preferred when known.

pin.description: Self-check-in PIN; shown in a detail modal.

note.description: Short highlight (e.g. 'Rezeption 24/7, Tesla SC am Hotel').

status.description: Defaults to 'confirmed' if omitted. 'candidate' renders muted until chosen. 'completed' marks a place that has already been visited.

Charger.description: Reusable EV charger entity. Referenced from Stop.charger_id to avoid duplicating per-drive.

name.description: Display name, e.g. 'Tesla Supercharger Magliano Sabina'.

network.description: Roaming/network identifier if different from operator (rare). Free-form.

kw.description: Peak power in kW per stall.

note.description: Short operator note (amenities, payment, layout quirks).

StayWeather.description: Forecast bucket for one stay. Days outside the forecast window (Open-Meteo: ~7 days ahead) are simply absent.

updated_at.description: When this bucket was last refreshed by the weather Action (UTC).

coords.description: Coordinates the forecast was fetched for (typically the stay coords).

days.description: Per-date forecast. Keys are ISO calendar dates (YYYY-MM-DD).

WeatherDay.description: Daily forecast summary. All fields optional — the Action writes what the provider returned.

maps_url.description: Deprecated when google_place_id is set. Generic Apple/Google Maps URL with ?q= text query — fallback for entries without a Google Place ID.

DateTime.description: ISO-8601 datetime, typically without seconds (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM) or with Z suffix for UTC.

GooglePlaceId.description: Google Maps Place ID (e.g. 'ChIJt-2vureqahMRXJ7AVYPun6E'). Build the rich Place-card URL via the official Google Maps URL API: https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=<NAME>&query_place_id=<id>. The 'query' parameter is required (display label, URL-encode it), 'query_place_id' triggers the Place-card lookup. This format works in the Google Maps mobile app AND the web — the older /maps/place/?q=place_id:<id> form is web-only and shows 'no results' in the app. Authoritative coords/photos/hours/reviews come from Google Places API. When set, prefer it over maps_url.
